exercises.title: Putting words together
exercises.body: Shavian has no capital letters — instead, a small dot before a word marks it as a name (like ·Sam). Otherwise sentences read just like English, word by word, only spelled the way they sound. 𐑞 𐑒𐑨𐑑 𐑕𐑨𐑑 reads as "the cat sat".

exercises.title: A few little words
exercises.body: A handful of common words get their own fixed spelling worth memorizing: [[I]] is just 𐑲 and [[a]] is 𐑩. The very commonest words shrink to a single letter: [[the]] is 𐑞, [[and]] is 𐑯, and [[to]] is 𐑑. You'll run into them constantly.

exercises.prompt: Which word means “a”, as in “a big cat”?
